seoTitle: How to Delete a Microsoft Loop Component (2026)
metaDescription: Remove a shared Loop table, task list, or tracker from a page, Teams chat, or Outlook message, and recover it from the recycle bin if needed.
intro: Dead or outdated Loop components left sitting in a chat or page can clutter your workspace and confuse anyone still interacting with them. This guide covers finding the block's hidden option handle, deleting it from a page or a Teams/Outlook message, and restoring it if you delete something by mistake.

steps.heading: Locate the component
steps.body: Open your Loop page canvas or chat feed and navigate to the target component you want to remove.

steps.heading: Reveal the option handle
steps.body: Hover your cursor over the top edge of the component box to reveal the six-dot drag handle icon.

steps.heading: Open the context menu
steps.body: Click the six-dot handle to open the centralized context utility dropdown.

steps.heading: Delete the component
steps.body: Select Delete from the menu to remove the live block directly from your page canvas.

steps.heading: Delete from a chat if needed
steps.body: If the component is inline inside a Teams or Outlook message, click the chat's more options and choose Delete to clear it there.

steps.heading: Restore if needed
steps.body: Open your workspace's Recycle Bin menu and select Restore to bring back an accidentally deleted component.

faq.question: Does deleting a component from a chat also delete the source file?
faq.answer: No, deleting the inline block from a chat removes it from that view, which is different from permanently purging the underlying file in OneDrive or SharePoint.

faq.question: Can I get a deleted component back?
faq.answer: Yes, the integrated Loop Recycle Bin lets you restore recently deleted components within a limited window.

faq.question: Why don't I see a delete option on a component?
faq.answer: This is usually caused by restricted workspace access settings — check with an admin if the delete command appears grayed out.

faq.question: Will removing a component affect the layout around it?
faq.answer: The surrounding section should auto-collapse neatly once a component is deleted, but it's worth double-checking your layout afterward.
